grounding_note: Row 332 ('wrongfully terminated ... for telling my boss co workers were being negligent ... knowing I have dislexia') split humans between Wrongful Discharge and ADA/Discrimination/Whistleblowers. The consensus analysis found the taxonomy encodes the unlawful MOTIVE, not the firing itself. Users lead with 'I was fired unfairly' and hold back the motive fact; disclosing it selects the exact employment route. Under FETCH's runtime taxonomy the employee-side labels are used because the user is unambiguously the worker in every opening.

scenarios.opening_query: I was fired last week for no good reason after six years and I want to know if it was legal.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: Two weeks before, I reported to the state that my company was dumping waste illegally.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: This happened two weeks after I reported our illegal waste dumping to the state.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the firing followed the employee reporting illegal activity
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I was fired the day after I told my boss I wouldn't falsify the delivery logs he asked me to change.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was the day after I refused to falsify the delivery logs like my boss wanted.
scenarios.vetting_note: Reporting illegal activity = Whistleblowers; refusing to commit the act = Wrongful Discharge. Both are motive facts users omit from the first message.

scenarios.opening_query: My employer let me go out of nowhere and gave a reason that makes no sense. Can they do that?
scenarios.final: Labor & Employment > ADA (Disability) Act Issues - Employee
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: It came a week after I asked for a modified schedule for my multiple sclerosis, which HR has documented.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was a week after I asked HR for a modified schedule for my MS — they have it documented.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a disability or accommodation request preceded the termination
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It came right after I filed a workers' comp claim for my shoulder injury.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It came right after I filed my workers' comp claim for my shoulder.
scenarios.vetting_note: Accommodation-request timing routes to ADA; comp-retaliation stays in Wrongful Discharge per the taxonomy description.

scenarios.opening_query: After ten years with great reviews I suddenly got terminated. I think it was completely unfair.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: My new manager had been making comments about my age and replaced me with someone in their twenties.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: My manager kept making comments about my age, and they replaced me with someone in their twenties.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a protected characteristic like age motivated the termination
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It happened the week I got back from jury duty that my boss had pressured me to get out of.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was the week I got back from jury duty — my boss had pushed me to get out of serving.
scenarios.vetting_note: Age plus replacement-by-younger-worker is the canonical discrimination disclosure; jury-service retaliation is the taxonomy's own Wrongful Discharge example.

scenarios.opening_query: I got fired from the care facility where I worked and I'm sure it wasn't for the reason they claim.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: I had just told the administrator that staff were neglecting residents, and I put it in writing.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I had just reported in writing that staff were neglecting residents.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the employee reported misconduct or unsafe conditions before the firing
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It happened two days after I refused to work a shift alone in a way that would break the facility's license rules.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was two days after I refused to staff a shift alone in violation of our license rules.
scenarios.vetting_note: Directly modeled on row 332 (homeless shelter negligence report); time-sensitive 30/90-day whistleblower deadlines make routing matter.

scenarios.opening_query: Terminated yesterday with no warning. I have a mortgage and kids and I feel this was wrong.
scenarios.final: Labor & Employment > ADA (Disability) Act Issues - Employee
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: It was the day I returned from epilepsy-related medical leave; my supervisor said they need someone 'reliable'.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was the day I came back from leave for my epilepsy — my supervisor said they need someone 'reliable'.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the firing connects to a medical condition or leave
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It came hours after I told payroll I would report them if they kept docking our overtime illegally — but I hadn't reported anyone yet, I just refused to sign off on it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was right after I refused to sign off on the illegal overtime docking.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'Reliable' comment after disability leave is a strong ADA disclosure grounded in real intake language.

scenarios.opening_query: My boss fired me and I know the official reason is fake. What can I do about it?
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: It happened a month after I told HR I was pregnant, and my duties were fine until then.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was about a month after I told HR I was pregnant — everything was fine before that.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether pregnancy or another protected status preceded the adverse action
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It happened right after I testified against the company in a coworker's wage lawsuit under subpoena.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was right after I testified under subpoena in my coworker's wage case.
scenarios.vetting_note: Pregnancy timing is a discrimination disclosure users frequently withhold at first contact.

scenarios.opening_query: I was pushed out of my job after raising concerns internally. They called it a restructuring.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: The concerns were about Medicare billing fraud, and I had also called the state hotline about it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The concerns were Medicare billing fraud — I'd also called the state hotline.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: what the raised concerns were about and whether they were reported externally
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The concerns were about my own unpaid final commissions, and they cut me the day after I hired a lawyer to demand them.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: My concerns were my own unpaid commissions — they cut me the day after my demand letter.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'Raising concerns' is ambiguous between protected whistleblowing and a personal pay dispute until the subject is disclosed.

scenarios.opening_query: Fired after 14 years. My manager never liked me and finally found an excuse. Is there anything I can do?
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: He repeatedly mocked my accent and told me to 'speak American' in front of customers.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He constantly mocked my accent and told me to 'speak American' in front of customers.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the manager's hostility targeted national origin or another protected trait
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The 'excuse' came right after I refused his direct order to bypass the safety guard on the panel saw.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was after I refused his order to bypass the saw guard.
scenarios.vetting_note: Personal-animus framing hides national-origin discrimination until the specific comments are disclosed.

scenarios.opening_query: Lost my warehouse job right before my benefits were supposed to kick in. Feels retaliatory.
scenarios.final: Labor & Employment > ADA (Disability) Act Issues - Employee
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: It was the same week I gave them my doctor's note asking for a lifting restriction for my back condition.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was the week I turned in my doctor's note asking for a lifting restriction.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a medical restriction or accommodation request preceded the firing
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was the same week I told the foreman I was going to report our missing guardrails to the state if he didn't fix them, and I refused to keep working the unguarded mezzanine.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was the week I refused to keep working the unguarded mezzanine.
scenarios.vetting_note: Doctor's-note timing is the accommodation fact; the 'benefits' red herring keeps the opening ambiguous.

scenarios.opening_query: My hospital employer terminated me and I believe it was retaliation, not the performance issue they invented.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: I had reported unsafe patient ratios to the nursing board a month earlier.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: A month before, I reported our unsafe patient ratios to the nursing board.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the retaliation followed a report to a regulator
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The retaliation started after I served on a grand jury for six weeks despite my director's objections.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It started after my six weeks of grand jury service my director resented.
scenarios.vetting_note: Modeled on row 41's ADA-violation reporting posture in a hospital; regulator report is the whistleblower trigger.

scenarios.opening_query: I think I was wrongfully terminated from my retail job and I want to talk to an employment lawyer.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: The scheduling manager cut my hours to zero after I wore my hijab to work despite his 'dress code' warnings.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It started when I wore my hijab to work — the manager called it a dress code issue and zeroed my hours.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether religious practice triggered the adverse treatment
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It happened after I refused to keep clocking out and finishing closing duties off the clock, and told him that's illegal.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was after I refused to keep doing closing duties off the clock.
scenarios.vetting_note: Religious-accommodation firing is Discrimination; refusal to work unpaid stays in the discharge/wage cluster.

scenarios.opening_query: Let go from my office job with a vague 'not a good fit' explanation after years of promotions.
scenarios.final: Labor & Employment > ADA (Disability) Act Issues - Employee
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: The change started after I disclosed my bipolar diagnosis to my team lead and asked for a quieter workspace.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Everything changed after I disclosed my bipolar diagnosis and asked for a quieter workspace.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a disclosed condition and accommodation request preceded the change in treatment
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The change started after I told my team lead I wouldn't sign backdated inspection reports.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It changed after I refused to sign backdated inspection reports.
scenarios.vetting_note: Mental-health disclosure is the exact 'employer knew about dyslexia' pattern in row 332.

scenarios.opening_query: My employer forced me to resign by making my job impossible. I refused to quit so they fired me.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'impossible' part was my shift lead's constant sexual comments and touching after I turned him down.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was my shift lead's constant sexual comments and touching after I turned him down.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the hostile treatment was sexual in nature
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They started the campaign the week after I filed my workers' comp claim for the loading-dock injury.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It all started the week after my workers' comp claim went in.
scenarios.vetting_note: Constructive-discharge framing conceals harassment until the conduct is described; grounded in row 344's mixed harassment complaint.

scenarios.opening_query: Fired from my county job and the union says I should also talk to a lawyer about why.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > Public Employees - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: I had just given the county auditor records showing my department was misusing grant money.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I'd just handed the county auditor proof my department was misusing grant funds.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the employee disclosed financial misconduct to oversight before the firing
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It came the day after I refused my supervisor's order to delete emails that were under a public-records request.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was the day after I refused to delete emails covered by a records request.
scenarios.vetting_note: Public-sector variant; auditor disclosure is whistleblowing, refusal-to-destroy is the discharge exception.

scenarios.opening_query: I was terminated right after returning from leave and I'm positive the timing wasn't a coincidence.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: It was approved FMLA leave to care for my dying mother, and they filled my position while I was out.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was approved FMLA leave for my dying mother — they filled my job while I was gone.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the leave was job-protected FMLA leave
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The leave was two days off for a court appearance as a subpoenaed witness against a company vendor.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The 'leave' was two subpoenaed days testifying against one of our vendors.
scenarios.vetting_note: Rows 335/341 show FMLA vs. general employment is a live human boundary; leave type is the disclosure.

scenarios.opening_query: I reported problems at work months ago and nothing happened to me then, but now I've been fired.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Whistleblowers - Employee || Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: The firing actually came the day after I filed a workers' comp claim; the old report was resolved amicably.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Honestly the firing came the day after my workers' comp claim — the old report had been resolved fine.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which event actually preceded and triggered the termination
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Last week the state investigator I'd talked to showed up on site, and I was fired the next morning.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The state investigator I talked to showed up last week — I was gone the next morning.
scenarios.vetting_note: Tests whether the pipeline can move OFF the whistleblower default when the operative trigger is comp retaliation.

scenarios.opening_query: They fired me for 'performance' but my numbers were the best on the team. Something else is going on.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: I'm the only Black manager they've ever had, and the VP had questioned whether clients were 'comfortable' with me.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I was their only Black manager, and the VP had asked whether clients were 'comfortable' with me.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether race or another protected characteristic is behind the pretext
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The 'something else' is that I had just declined to help the VP hide rebates from our biggest client's audit and told him it was fraud I wouldn't touch.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I had just refused to help the VP hide rebates from a client audit.
scenarios.vetting_note: Pretext framing with best-on-team numbers; race disclosure is the pivotal fact.

scenarios.opening_query: I got laid off but I was the only one 'laid off' and they posted my job again a month later.
scenarios.final: Labor & Employment > ADA (Disability) Act Issues - Employee
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'layoff' came a month after my cancer diagnosis became known in the office.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It came about a month after word of my cancer diagnosis got around the office.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a serious health condition became known before the sham layoff
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It came a month after I testified for a former coworker at her unemployment hearing against the company.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was a month after I testified for a former coworker at her unemployment hearing.
scenarios.vetting_note: Sham-layoff pattern; diagnosis disclosure moves it to ADA.

scenarios.opening_query: Terminated from my trucking job and I'm certain the reason they wrote up is a cover story.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: I had reported the company to federal safety regulators for forcing drivers past their legal hours.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I'd reported them to the federal safety regulators for pushing us past legal driving hours.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a regulator report about safety violations preceded the firing
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I had refused dispatch's order to drive past my legal hours that same night, and they wrote me up the next day.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I refused dispatch's order to drive past my legal hours — the write-up came the next day.
scenarios.vetting_note: Report-vs-refuse is the cleanest minimal pair in this family: same underlying violation, different exact label.

scenarios.opening_query: My employer fired me two months before my pension vested. I gave that company everything.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Labor & Employment > Wrongful Discharge - Employee || Labor & Employment > General - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: HR emails I saved show they tracked my vesting date and timed the termination to avoid paying the pension.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I have HR emails tracking my vesting date — they timed the firing to dodge the pension.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the termination was timed to defeat retirement benefits
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The firing came the week after I refused my supervisor's demand that I punch his timecard for hours he wasn't there.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It came after I refused to keep punching my supervisor's timecard for him.
scenarios.vetting_note: ERISA Section 510 benefit-interference is a distinct exact route the vesting-timing fact reveals.
